data.title: Buy Rating Backed by Strong FY25 Recovery, Robust FY26 Pipeline, and Balance Sheet Strengthening via Library Disposal
data.description:
In a report released yesterday, Giada Cabrino from Intesa Sanpaolo assigned a Buy rating on Leone Film Group SpA, with a price target of €2.50.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Giada Cabrino has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that highlight both recent execution and future potential. The company delivered solid FY25 results, with the Production unit expanding revenues and profitability thanks in part to the success of “Follemente,” which restored positive EBIT and brought net income close to break-even after the prior year’s loss.

Looking ahead, Cabrino expects FY26 to improve further, supported by a strong production slate, a higher number of theatrical releases in Distribution, and healthier overall revenue momentum. He also views the partial disposal of the non-current library as a strategically sound move that strengthens the balance sheet and supports a revised target price of EUR 2.5, implying attractive upside from current levels.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: HIVE Digital: Early-Mover AI Cloud Expansion, Low-Cost Renewable Power, and Paraguay Optionality Support Buy Rating
data.description:
Analyst Joseph Vafi from Canaccord Genuity maintained a Buy rating on HIVE Digital Technologies and keeping the price target at $10.00.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Joseph Vafi has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that highlight HIVE’s strategic positioning and growth potential. He underscores that HIVE moved early to redeploy legacy GPUs into an AI services platform, creating a dual-engine model of fast-growing AI cloud revenue alongside a still highly profitable Bitcoin mining operation. This diversified approach is complemented by a differentiated power strategy that secures access to abundant, low-cost, renewable energy.

Vafi also emphasizes the emerging Paraguay opportunity, which he likens to the early-stage West Texas power boom for data centers, noting HIVE’s 400 MW power footprint tied to the Itaipu hydro complex and its ability to shift capacity from mining to higher-value AI workloads over time. He points to HIVE’s partnership with leading telecom provider Tigo to deliver Tier III AI cloud services into the U.S. as evidence of execution, while the strong demand for the recent $115 million zero-coupon convertible note signals rising institutional confidence. Collectively, these elements support his conviction that HIVE is evolving into a more institutional-quality platform with meaningful upside.

According to TipRanks, Vafi is a 5-star analyst with an average return of 24.9% and a 46.82% success rate. Vafi covers the Technology sector, focusing on stocks such as Circle Internet Group, Inc. Class A, Strategy, and Block.

In another report released on April 9, Cantor Fitzgerald also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $3.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
